summary: Second matching pass for `src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c`, adding a large amount of C coverage for collision math and hit/hurt capsule routines, renaming many callsites from generic `func_8000....`/`HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr` symbols to `lbColl_*`, and tightening collision-related headers/types used by fighter, item, and baselib code. The PR also added `tools/report_score.py` to generate asm-differ score tables for object functions; the author noted the max score was broken in the posted report.
what_changed: Added roughly 2k lines to `src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c`, including C or guarded-asm implementations/prototypes for many `lbColl_*` routines such as `lbColl_80005C44`, `lbColl_80005EBC`, `lbColl_80005FC0`, `lbColl_80006094`, `lbColl_800067F8`, `lbColl_80006E58`, `lbColl_800077A0`, `lbColl_80007AFC`, `lbColl_80007BCC`, `lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix`, `lbColl_80007ECC`, `lbColl_8000805C`, `lbColl_80008248`, `lbColl_800083C4`, `lbColl_80008428`, `lbColl_80008434`, `lbColl_80008440`, `lbColl_CopyHitCapsule`, `lbColl_80008688`, `lbColl_8000A244`, `lbColl_8000A95C`, and `lbColl_8000ACFC`.

Reworked `lbcollision.h` from loose `func_8000....` declarations and older `Hitbox`/`Hurtbox` naming into more specific collision types: `HitResult`, `HitVictim`, `HitCapsule`, and `HurtCapsule`, with fields like `a_offset`, `b_offset`, `scl`, `bone`, `a_pos`, `b_pos`, `bone_idx`, `kind`, and `is_grabbable`.

Renamed the old `HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr` call target to `lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix` at many assembly and C callsites, including eflib, fighter animation/collision code, lb background flash, and sysdolphin baselib `robj.s` callsites.

Updated many assembly callsites across fighter and item code from raw `func_8000....` names to `lbColl_*` names, for example `func_800084FC` to `lbColl_CopyHitCapsule`, `func_80007ECC` to `lbColl_80007ECC`, `func_8000ACFC` to `lbColl_8000ACFC`, and `func_80008D30` to `lbColl_80008D30`.
Adjusted fighter and item source/header files to use `HitCapsule`, `HurtCapsule`, and `lbColl_*` APIs instead of older or generic names.

Added `tools/report_score.py`, which iterates text symbols from an object file with `nm`, runs `tools/asm-differ/diff.py --format json` for each symbol, filters perfect matches, sorts by percent, and prints a markdown score table.
smart_moves: The PR paired matching work with systematic symbol propagation: once lbcollision functions were identified, dependent asm/source callsites were renamed in the same change, making future cross-references searchable by `lbColl_*` instead of anonymous `func_8000....` names.

The collision structs were made more semantically useful without over-claiming every field: known capsule endpoints and positions gained names like `a_offset`, `b_offset`, `a_pos`, `b_pos`, while uncertain bytes remained as `unk_*`/bitfield placeholders.

Using `HitCapsule` and `HurtCapsule` terminology clarifies that these structures model capsule collision volumes, not generic hitboxes/hurtboxes, which helps later matching in fighter and item collision code.
The PR retained guarded `asm` versions for difficult routines while adding C equivalents or partial equivalents, allowing incremental matching without blocking progress on unresolved functions.

The score-report script made it easier to prioritize remaining nonmatching functions by showing function-level asm-differ scores, even though the posted PR text says the max score calculation/report was broken.
decomp_lessons: When a library module starts matching, rename callsites immediately across asm and C. This PR shows that even unchanged machine code becomes more useful once calls like `func_800084FC` become `lbColl_CopyHitCapsule` and `HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr` becomes `lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix`.

Collision data recovery benefits from endpoint-based field names. For capsule-like structs, prefer explicit pairs such as `a_offset`/`b_offset` and `a_pos`/`b_pos` over vague numbered vectors once usage supports it.
Do not force full semantic names for unknown fields. This PR mixes strong names for confirmed fields with `unk_34`, `unk_41`, and bitfield placeholders where evidence is weaker.
Incremental matching can combine C implementations, inline helpers, and `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO asm` stubs in one file as long as prototypes and types are made consistent for dependent code.
Function-level score reports are useful for PR review and follow-up planning, but generated metrics should be treated cautiously when the author flags a known reporting issue.
naming_conventions: Collision library symbols use the `lbColl_` prefix, keeping address-suffixed names where behavior is not fully understood, e.g. `lbColl_8000805C`, `lbColl_80008248`, and `lbColl_8000ACFC`.
A known behavior can replace an address suffix when evidence is strong, as with `lbColl_CopyHitCapsule`.
`HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr` was renamed to `lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix`, indicating this matrix setup helper belongs with lb collision usage rather than being left as an unknown baselib JObj helper.
Struct names moved from broad `Hitbox`/`Hurtbox` terminology toward `HitCapsule`/`HurtCapsule` for capsule collision volumes.
Recovered vector fields use endpoint and role names such as `a_offset`, `b_offset`, `a_pos`, `b_pos`, `scl`, `bone`, `bone_idx`, `kind`, and `is_grabbable`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Widespread asm-only callsite relabeling was used to keep disassembly synchronized with newly identified lbcollision symbols without changing instructions.
Several hard functions were kept as `asm` declarations guarded for MWERKS/GEKKO while C versions and prototypes were introduced nearby, supporting staged matching.
The lbcollision math routines use explicit temporary vector/dot-product style C, which is a common tactic for matching PowerPC floating-point code where expression ordering matters.
Inline helpers such as `approximatelyZero`, `testPlusX`, `testPlus`, and `testMinusX` were introduced in the lbcollision area, likely to control repeated comparison logic and improve matchability.
`tools/report_score.py` automated asm-differ JSON scoring over all text symbols in an object to identify remaining mismatches and rank them by percent.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the dump.
One review from `r-burns` approved the PR with an empty body on 2023-02-01.
The only substantive human note in the PR body was from the author: "Ok the max score is broken I don't feel like fixing it right now," referring to the posted score report.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish matching remaining `lbColl_80008D30`, `lbColl_80009DD4`, `lbColl_8000A584`, or other `UNK_RET lbColl_*` routines introduced here.
Search later history for additional renames of `HitCapsule` and `HurtCapsule` fields, especially remaining `unk_*` and bitfields in `lbcollision.h`.
Check whether `tools/report_score.py` was later fixed or replaced due to the max-score issue mentioned in the PR body.
Find later PRs that remove the guarded `asm` stubs from `src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c`.
